title: Labels like 'If yes' get rewritten with parent context when the parent isn't visually adjacent
description: When a level-2 source (Case Book) provides a label that is meaningful only in immediate proximity to its parent question (e.g. 'If yes' next to a parent 'Ventricular Tachycardia' question), and the parent question is not visually adjacent in the rendered form (because the gated field appears later, after a relevant evaluates), the label is rewritten to be self-explanatory. DETECTION: the skill flags a label for rewrite when (a) the label is one of 'If yes', 'If yes:', 'If yes, ', 'If applicable', 'If other' (with optional trailing punctuation), AND (b) the field has a relevant clause referencing a parent field, AND (c) the parent field's label provides domain context (e.g. 'Ventricular Tachycardia'). AUTO-APPLIED BEHAVIOR: rewrite the label to incorporate parent context — 'If yes, type of VT' (with appropriate brevity), 'If yes, type of AFL'. Section numbering from the Case Book is preserved (e.g. '1.5.1. If yes, type of VT'). EVERY REWRITE IS RECORDED in review_flags.protocol_ambiguous with form, field, case_book_section, case_book_label, skill_rewrite, rationale, and action_required. UNIVERSAL SCOPE per §0. Override: customer library uses the original label verbatim and the form is otherwise sourced from level 2; carry forward as-authored without flag — the §23 rewrite applies only when the skill is generating the label itself.
rationale: When 'If yes' appears in the source PDF immediately below its parent question, the connection is visually obvious to the form reader. When the same 'If yes' renders in OpenClinica after a relevant clause evaluates — often with other fields in between, or on a different page — the connection is lost. Data-entry coordinators see a question 'If yes' with no obvious parent and don't know what 'yes' to refers to. Rewriting the label to include parent context ('If yes, type of VT') makes the question self-explanatory regardless of where it renders in the form flow.

flag.message: Field ${field.name} on ${form.form_id} has minimal label '${field.label}' with a relevant clause. The parent question's domain context should be inlined into the label (e.g. 'If yes' → 'If yes, type of VT' for a parent 'Ventricular Tachycardia'). Rewrite preserves Case Book section numbering. Verify the rewrite matches sponsor intent before commit.

effect.soft: Identify the parent field referenced by this row's relevant clause. Read the parent's label (e.g. 'Ventricular Tachycardia') and rewrite this row's label to incorporate the parent's domain context: 'If yes' becomes 'If yes, type of VT' or 'If yes, specify VT type'. Preserve Case Book section numbering at the start of the label (e.g. '1.5.1. If yes, ...'). Record every rewrite in review_flags.protocol_ambiguous with form, field, case_book_section, original case_book_label, skill_rewrite, rationale='Hidden-parent-context disambiguation', and action_required='Verify rewrite matches sponsor intent.'

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §23 during Phase B.1d. Detection is structured (regex matches on the bare-minimum labels plus presence of a relevant clause). The actual rewrite requires reading the parent field's label and synthesizing a context-preserving new label — that's judgment-heavy and stays in soft. Relates to OC-7 sub-rules 7O.b/c/f (general relevance idioms) but is more specific: §23 fires on the exact label-text triggers and produces a structural label rewrite, while 7O.b/c/f document the general relevance-idiom catalog. Both coexist.
